task.title: Use Copilot Chat to Understand Your Code

prereq.p: GitHub Copilot must be installed and activated in VS Code.

context.p: Copilot Chat is a conversational interface inside VS Code. You type questions in plain language and Copilot replies with explanations or code suggestions tailored to the files you have open.

step.cmd: Open the Copilot Chat panel by clicking the chat icon in the VS Code Activity Bar, or press Ctrl + Alt + I (Windows) / Cmd + Alt + I (Mac).
Open the script file you want help with in the editor so Copilot has context.
Type your question in the chat input box. For example:

stepxmp.userinput: What does this script do, step by step?

step.cmd: Read Copilot's response. If you need clarification, type a follow-up question.
If Copilot suggests a code fix, click Apply in Editor to insert it, then save the file and run the script to test the fix.

result.p: You can now use Copilot Chat as an on-demand tutor for any code or error you encounter during the course.
